identitySentence: Youngberg Law Firm is a Denton family law practice where founding attorney Michael Youngberg, licensed in Texas since 2014 and drawing on his background in the Texas Attorney General's child support division, personally guides Denton County clients through divorce, custody, support, adoption, and protective order matters.

concept.headline: A Denton Family Law Attorney Who Handles Your Case Personally
concept.subheadline: Divorce, custody, support, or adoption questions rarely arrive at a convenient moment. Share what is happening in your family, and Michael Youngberg will review it himself and meet with you to explain your options under Texas law.
credibilityPoints: Licensed to practice law in Texas since 2014
Super Lawyers Rising Stars selection, 2021 through 2026
Prior experience in the Texas Attorney General's child support division
Member of the Denton County Bar Association and the College of the State Bar of Texas

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Youngberg Law Firm practices family law across Denton County, including divorce, child custody, child support cases and modifications, property division, spousal maintenance, adoption, paternity, mediation, and enforcement of existing court orders. The firm also assists clients facing domestic violence, including temporary restraining orders and protective orders.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm is based at 2516 Lillian Miller Pkwy, Suite 110 in Denton and focuses exclusively on Denton County families, serving clients in Denton, Flower Mound, Highland Village, and Little Elm with ready access to the Denton County courts.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: When you reach out, you will be asked a few straightforward questions about your situation so the consultation time is used well. You will meet directly with Michael Youngberg, who will explain how Texas family law applies to your circumstances and outline realistic next steps, with no pressure and no obligation.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the Youngberg Law Firm team,

Your firm stands out in our research for a specific reason: it is a true solo practice where clients work directly with Michael Youngberg, a Texas-licensed attorney since 2014 with a background in the Texas Attorney General's child support division, serving Denton County families exclusively.

That personal model is exactly why a Lead Capturer fits your practice. Family law inquiries, whether about divorce, custody, child support, adoption, or a protective order, often arrive late at night or on weekends, from people who are anxious and comparing several firms at once. Your website already does a great deal right: clear consultation calls to action, online booking, and a 24/7 answering service. What it cannot do on its own is read each inquiry, respond to the specific topic immediately, and convert that moment of urgency into a reserved appointment while you are in court or with a client. Your current contact form is also a third-party JavaScript embed, which means anyone whose browser blocks it quietly disappears.

We have already built your Lead Capturer for you: a custom identity sentence and a custom contact form written specifically for your firm. Here is the sentence we drafted from your verified record:

"Youngberg Law Firm is a Denton family law practice where founding attorney Michael Youngberg, licensed in Texas since 2014 and drawing on his background in the Texas Attorney General's child support division, personally guides Denton County clients through divorce, custody, support, adoption, and protective order matters."

Here is how it works. Every inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox, so nothing sits in a dashboard you have to remember to check. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to the topic of their message, whether that is custody, child support, divorce, or adoption. That response presents your genuinely available consultation times. A $50 reservation deposit then confirms the appointment, so the people on your calendar have committed to showing up.

We are offering a 15-day proof period. We would rather you test the complete experience and judge the results than take our word for anything. We make no revenue promises; the point of the proof period is that you can watch real inquiries move through the system yourself.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter any confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm that response is sent to the customer email without any action from your staff.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm the response addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will make it right before asking anything further of you.

Respectfully,

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
